heading: Assessment of environmental impact of proposed multilateral development bank actions
label: 262m–7 Assessment of environmental impact of proposed multilateral development bank actions
notes: References in TextThis title, referred to in subsec. (g), is title XIII of , which is classified to sections 262m to 262m–7 of this title. For complete classification of title XIII to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 95–118

children.heading: Assessment required before favorable vote on proposal
children.label: a Assessment required before favorable vote on proposal

children.text: an assessment analyzing the environmental impacts of the proposed action, including associated and cumulative impacts, and of alternatives to the proposed action, has been completed by the borrower or the bank and has been made available to the board of directors of the bank; and

such assessment or a comprehensive summary of the assessment (with proprietary information redacted) has been made available to affected groups, and local nongovernmental organizations and notice of its availability in the country and at the bank has been posted on the bank’s website.

children.heading: Access to assessments in all member countries
children.label: b Access to assessments in all member countries
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall seek the adoption of policies and procedures, through discussions and negotiations with the other member countries of the multilateral development banks and with the management of such banks, which result in access by governmental agencies and interested members of the public of such member countries, to environmental assessments or documentary information containing comprehensive summaries of such assessments which discuss the environmental impact of prospective projects and programs being considered by such banks. Such assessments or summaries should be made available to such governmental agencies and interested members of the public at least 120 days before scheduled board action, and public participation in review of the relevant environmental information should be encouraged.
ensure that an environmental impact assessment or comprehensive summary of such assessment described in subsection (a) accompanies loan proposals through the agency review process; and
take into consideration recommendations from all other interested Federal agencies and interested members of the public.

children.heading: Development of procedures for systematic environmental assessment
children.label: d Development of procedures for systematic environmental assessment

children.text: instruct the United States Executive Director of each multilateral development bank to initiate discussions with the other executive directors of the respective bank and to propose that the respective bank develop and make available to member governments of, and borrowers from, the respective bank, within 18 months after , a procedure for the systematic environmental assessment of development projects for which the respective bank provides financial assistance, taking into consideration the Guidelines and Principles for Environmental Impact Assessment promulgated by the United Nations Environmental Programme and other bilateral or multilateral assessment procedures; andDecember 19, 1989

children.label: A
children.text: the environmental impact assessment of the action described in subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: interagency and public review of such assessment; and

children.label: C
children.text: other environmental review and consultation of such action that is required by other law.

children.heading: Use of United States personnel
children.label: e Use of United States personnel
A
children.text: training bank staff in environmental impact assessment procedures;

children.label: C
children.text: preparing environmental studies for projects with potentially significant environmental impacts; and

children.label: D
children.text: preparing documents for public release, and developing procedures to provide for the inclusion of interested nongovernmental organizations in the environmental review process; and
encourage other member countries of such banks to provide similar assistance.

children.label: A
children.text: not later than the end of the 1-year period beginning on , a progress report on the efficacy of efforts by the United States to encourage consistent and timely environmental impact assessment of actions proposed to be taken by the multilateral development banks and on the progress made by the multilateral development banks in developing and instituting environmental assessment policies and procedures; andDecember 19, 1989

children.label: B
children.text: not later than , a detailed report on the matters described in subparagraph (A).January 1, 1993

children.heading: Availability of reports
children.label: 2 Availability of reports
children.text: The reports required by paragraph (1) shall be made available to the member governments of, and the borrowers from, the multilateral development banks, and to the public.

children.heading: Multilateral development bank defined
children.label: g Multilateral development bank defined
children.text: In this title, the term “multilateral development bank” means the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development, the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development, the International Development Association, the International Finance Corporation, the Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency, the African Development Bank, the African Development Fund, the Asian Development Bank, the Inter-American Development Bank, the Inter-American Investment Corporation, any other institution (other than the International Monetary Fund) specified in , and any subsidiary of any such institution.1section 262r(c)(2) of this titleSee References in Text note below.1
